title: Guajava oblongata
taxonomy: Guajava oblongata is a species of Guava native to tropical Asia. It belongs to the family Myrtaceae. The genus Guajava is a part of the order Myrtales. Guava species are deciduous or evergreen trees or shrubs with showy flowers and edible fruits. Guajava oblongata is a small to medium-sized tree that grows in tropical and subtropical regions.
uses: Guajava oblongata, also known as the strawberry tree or strawberry guava, has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits. The plant's leaves and fruits are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. In some cultures, the plant is used to treat digestive issues, such as diarrhea and stomach ulcers, due to its anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's leaves are also used to treat fever and infections, as well as to reduce pain and inflammation. Additionally, the plant's extracts are being studied for their potential anti-cancer and neuroprotective effects.
benefits: Guajava oblongata, also known as the Guava tree, has been widely studied for its potential health benefits. properties: Guajava oblongata is known to have various therapeutic properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. constituents: Guajava oblongata contains various active constituents, including flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant's flavonoids, such as kaempferol and quercetin, have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. Additionally, Guajava oblongata contains triterpenoids and saponins, which have been reported to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities. The plant's alkaloids, including berberine and palmatine, have been found to have antimicrobial and anti-diarrheal effects. Further research is needed to fully understand the potential benefits and mechanisms of action of Guajava oblongata's active constituents.

preparations: Guajava oblongata is often used to make herbal preparations that aid in digestive issues and relieve symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome. intro: The Guajava oblongata, also known as the Guava tree, is a tropical plant native to Central and South America, characterized by its sweet and slightly sour fruit. The Guava tree has been used in traditional medicine for its numerous health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which have been shown to aid in the treatment of digestive issues, wounds, and fever. The bioactive constituents of Guava include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Guava is commonly used in herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as in the form of jams, jellies, and extracts, which are used to support immune function, reduce inflammation, and promote overall well-being.
